- Row 13: BBBBBRBBB - wall with red pixel at col 40
- Rows 14-19: WWWWBRB - white space with red pixel at col 40 and wall at col 39 and 41
So the red path is a vertical line at col 40, from rows 9-40 (approximately). The player is at rows 8-12, cols 38-42, which is above the red path.
The player can't move DOWN because there's a wall at row 13, cols 38-42.
Wait, but the red path is at col 40, which is inside the player's column range (38-42). The player overlaps with the red path at the top.
Let me reconsider the game mechanic. The red path is a "trail" that the player needs to follow. The player starts at the top-left and needs to move along the maze corridors to reach the target at the bottom-right.
But the player can't move DOWN from the top-right position. The only way to move DOWN is from the top-left position (rows 8-12, cols 14-19).
So the player needs to move LEFT from the top-right position back to the top-left position, then move DOWN along the left corridor.

Let me try a different approach: move LEFT from the top-right position back to the top-left position, then move DOWN along the left corridor to the bottom, then move RIGHT along the bottom corridor to the target.
